column4: Niche record types to filter for
column5: Key fields to target in scraper

column4: Residential Pool, Residential Pool Reliner, Residential Reroof, Commercial Pool, Commercial Reroofaca-prod.accela
column5: Under General Search: Record Type, Start Date, End Date, Project Name, plus address fields (Street #, Street Name, etc.). Fill Record Type with the pool/roof values and bound Start/End Date to your timeframe.aca-prod.accela

column4: Pool Permit, Re-Roof Permit
column5: Use Search Applications → General Search: Record Number, Street Number (From/To), Street Name, Parcel Number as shown in the county’s ACA user guide; combine with Start Date/End Date if present to pull recent permits.aca-prod.accela+1

column4: Residential Roof, Residential Pool
column5: In Search Permit Records / Search Applications: use Record Number when known, but for lead gen rely on Record Type, Date Range, and Site Address / Parcel Number fields to pull pools and reroofs for the last 7 days.aca-prod.accela+1

column4: Residential Roof Trade Permit, Commercial Roof Trade Permit, Residential Miscellaneous Permit
column5: On Search for Records: target Record Type (set to pool/roof values), and date fields (Start Date, End Date) along with Site Address / Parcel Number to constrain the pull.aca-prod.accela

column4: Residential Pool Permit, Residential Roof Trade Permit
column5: Use Building Search for Records: pre‑filter Record Type to the pool/roof options, then set Start Date/End Date and optionally filter by address or project name to keep result volume manageable.

column4: Pool/Spa - Residential, Commercial Re-Roof, Residential Roofing or similar.aca-prod.accela
column5: Under General Search: populate Record Type, Start Date, End Date; optionally include Street #, Street Name, and Parcel No. to constrain area. This page explicitly shows all of these fields.aca-prod.accela

column4: Residential Pool/Spa Permit, Commercial Pool/Spa Permit, Residential Roofing Permit, Commercial Roofing Permit.aca-prod.accela
column5: Use General Search: set Record Type to the pool/roof entries and fill Start Date and End Date; optionally use Site Address fields for zip/area targeting.aca-prod.accela

column4: Residential pool/roof permits (filter by Record Type and/or description containing “Pool” or “Roof”).aca-prod.accela
column5: On Search Records → Building Records: use Start Date (explicitly mentioned) and Permit Number or Name of Business if you need contractor focus. For lead scraping, primarily set Start Date/End Date and leave address wide.aca-prod.accela

column4: Building/permits where Record Type or description includes “Roof” or “Pool”; OKC uses Accela standard modules.
column5: Use General Search under Permits: Site Address, Record Number, Record Type, and the date range fields (the portal notes you can adjust the default 12‑year window and that records go back to 1983). Filter by Record Type + Start/End Date.access.okc

column4: Building permits with pool/roof record types or descriptions.
column5: Use Advanced Search → Search Records/Applications → Building: Record Type, Date Range, and address (House Number/Street Name) are supported; the county explicitly documents searching by record type and date range via Accela.mymanatee

column3: Building permit status page: https://www.martin.fl.us/martin-county-services/building-permit-status → “Online permit system (ACA Portal)”martin
column4: Building permits for pools and reroofs (filter by Record Type or description after entering the ACA portal).
column5: Inside the ACA portal, follow Advanced Search → Search Records/Applications → Building and target Record Type, Start Date, End Date, and address fields; the county notes you can search without login using record number, house number/street, or contractor.martin

column4: Residential building permits where description or category contains “Pool”, “Swimming Pool”, “Reroof”, “Roof”.
column5: From PERMITTING → SEARCH FOR PERMIT: scrape by permit/record number, property address, and issue date; target filters that expose date range and text search over permit description or type. Citizenserve portals surface these in standard HTML forms.citizenserve

column2: Seminole (Yavapai install is county; this one is a city using Citizenserve)
column3: Citizenserve portal with installationID 441 (City of Seminole online portal) – same pattern as Yavapai’s, different installation ID.citizenserve
column4: Residential building permits with “Pool”, “Spa”, “Roof” in description/type.
column5: Use the SEARCH FOR PERMIT function: supply address or permit number and constrain by issue date; parse permit type/description text to isolate pool/roof jobs. Citizenserve renders results in HTML tables you can iterate over.citizenserve

column4: Building permits for pools and reroofs (no explicit type list, but pool/roof show up in descriptions and/or categories).
column5: On Click2Gov, target the permit search screen: fields typically include Permit Number, Site Address, and sometimes Application Date / Issue Date. Use the date field(s) plus address wildcard and then filter resulting rows for “ROOF”/“POOL” in the work description.semc-egov.aspgov

column4: Roofing, electrical, plumbing, remodeling permits etc., including pools; a public tutorial shows finding roofing permits here.youtubelkwo-egov.aspgov
column5: Use the building permit search: fields usually include Permit Number and Site Address. Since Click2Gov often lacks a direct “record type” dropdown, query recent permits by date (if available) or by wide address and then text‑filter descriptions for “Roof”, “Reroof”, “Pool”, “Spa”.youtubelkwo-egov.aspgov

column3: City permits page (front door to Accela): https://www.jurupavalley.org/406/Permitsjurupavalley
column4: Online Accela permit types include Re-Roof – Residential Reroof, Swimming Pools, plus other residential building permits.jurupavalley
column5: From this page follow the Accela Citizen Access link, then in Building Search for Records use Record Type (select reroof/swimming pool types) with Start Date/End Date and basic address to scrape new permits.jurupavalley

column3: Accela portal (linked in user guide): see ACA link inside PDF, then Building search.stancounty
column4: Residential pools and reroofs are specifically listed among permit types supported by ACA.stancounty
column5: In ACA Search Records/Applications → Building: use Record Type set to residential pool/roof options plus Date Range and address/parcel fields. The county notes you can research permits online via ACA.stancounty

column4: Roofing permits under “Roofing” section (Commercial or Residential Reroof) and related building permits.civicplus
column5: Once in ACA, use Building → Search Applications / Search for Records: target Record Type (Residential Reroof, Commercial Reroof etc.) and the date fields (Start Date, End Date), then capture address, owner, and valuation where exposed.civicplus

column4: Residential reroof plus swimming pool permits (forms mention “Residential Reroof Certificate of Compliance” and “Swimming Pools”).cityoflancasterca+1
column5: From the Accela home, open Building Search for Records: filter by Record Type (roof/pool categories) and Start Date/End Date; then scrape address, owner/contacts, and valuation fields from record detail pages.cityoflancasterca+1

column4: Residential building permits, including pools and reroofs in unincorporated areas and smaller communities.co-coconino-az.smartgovcommunity
column5: In SmartGov Public Portal → Search: target fields like Address, Parcel, and Issued Date or Applied Date; then filter returned records where permit type/description contains “Pool” or “Roof”. SmartGov exposes these via HTML grids similar to Accela.co-coconino-az.smartgovcommunity
